name: George Morrow
abstract: George Morrow (August 15, 1925 – May 26, 1992) was an American jazz double bassist. Although most closely associated with Max Roach and Clifford Brown, Morrow also appears on recordings by Sonny Rollins and Sonny Stitt.

concerts.label: Jazz and Variety Concert to Benefit The Lighthouse

works.t: Anything Goes: I Get a Kick Out of You

about_quotes.speaker: Earl Watkins
about_quotes.evidence: George Morrow, we had played together with the Jerome Richardson quartet.
about_quotes.doc_title: Earl Watkins interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 7, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Barry Harris
about_quotes.evidence: So that left Max and George Morrow, the bass player. So Donald Byrd, Sonny Rollins and myself, we joined Max’s band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Barry Harris — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Wilbur Ware
about_quotes.evidence: George and Brownie ... Brownie said, 'Man, I like your playing; come on by the hotel.' Well, naturally George Morrow and I hung out -- bass players, and I contacted Wilbur, and we played the Monday and Tuesdays.
about_quotes.doc_title: 68088_Wilbur B. Ware, Sr oral history interview
